text: Ever wonder how trees drink water from the ground? This process is called transpiration pull, and it’s more fascinating than you think. When water evaporates from leaves, it creates a vacuum. This vacuum helps pull more water from roots to leaves, sustaining the plant. So next time you see a tree, remember, it’s using science to stay alive. Curious for more? Hit like and follow for more science insights!
